original_abstract: Inspired by the synthesis of XB3C3 (X = Sr, La) compounds in the bipartite sodalite clathrate structure, density functional theory (DFT) calculations are performed on members of this family containing up to two different metal atoms. A DFT-chemical pressure analysis on systems with X = Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba reveals that the size of the metal cation, which can be tuned to stabilize the B-C framework, is key for their ambient-pressure dynamic stability. High-throughput density functional theory calculations on 105 Pm3̅ symmetry XYB6C6 binary-guest compounds (where X, Y are electropositive metal atoms) find 22 that are dynamically stable at 1 atm, expanding the number of potentially synthesizable phases by 19 (18 metals and 1 insulator). The density of states at the Fermi level and superconducting critical temperature, Tc, can be tuned by changing the average oxidation state of the metal atoms, with Tc being highest for an average valence of +1.5. KPbB6C6, with an ambient-pressure Eliashberg Tc of 88 K, is predicted to possess the highest Tc among the studied Pm3̅n XB3C3 or Pm3̅ XYB6C6 phases, and calculations suggest it may be synthesized using high-pressure high-temperature techniques and then quenched to ambient conditions.
humanized_abstract: Here, the possibility of synthesizing diverse amorphous boron carbide (aBC) precursors that upon reaction afford XB3C3 (sodalite clathrate structure, where X is a divalent or trivalent ion) was probed via density functional theory (DFT). For binary metal mixtures consisting of various divalent metal ions from Mg to Ba, a systematic DFT-chemical pressure study demonstrated the large impact of cation size on the optimal size of the BC framework that maintains structural integrity at ambient pressure. As part of the effort to address the challenges related to material prediction, high-throughput calculations involving 105 compounds in Pm3̅ symmetry with the formula XYB6C6 that correspond to different electropositive pairings of two metals were carried out. Among these binary-metal compounds, 22 compositions were predicted to be dynamically stable at 1 atm, thereby providing an additional 19 XX (insulating) synthesis targets, more than 6 times the current number of successfully synthesized members of this clathrate structural motif. Notably, some of these target compounds were observed to possess relatively large critical temperatures. Our DFT analysis showed that increasing the average charge of the two metal atoms in the binary framework led to higher critical temperatures, and such Tc was largest for an average valence of +1.5. Among all synthesized candidates, the ambiant-pressure Eliashberg critical temperature Tc of KPbB6C6 was calculated to be as high as 88 K, a value that is currently the highest in all studied members of the Pm3̅n XB3C3 and Pm3̅ XYB6C6 phases. Hence, synthesis of KPbB6C6 can potentially be performed using high pressure and high temperature, followed by quenching to ambient pressure and ambient temperature.
